features.explanation: This feature strongly activates on the Japanese comma punctuation mark '、' used to separate clauses or phrases.
features.token_pattern: the full-width Japanese comma character '、' (clause-separating punctuation)
features.context_pattern: appears at clause boundaries, often after particles like は、では、ですが、or at list/statement breaks
features.corpus_consistency: yes, it consistently fires on the same punctuation token across both flores and xsafety corpora

features.explanation: Fires on Japanese expressions of negation, opposition, or undesirability, especially tokens containing or implying "ない", "不", or explicit opposition like "反対".
features.token_pattern: peak tokens are negative morphemes or words indicating negation/opposition (e.g., ない, くない, しない, 不X, 反対, 不快, 不必要, sometimes volitional contrasts like したい)
features.context_pattern: sentences expressing refusal, avoidance, prohibition, opposition, lack, or negative evaluation, often in moral, legal, or safety-related discussions
features.safety_relevance: captures negation and intent language that often appears in harmful intent (e.g., 〜したい) or avoidance/prohibition contexts in safety scenarios
features.corpus_consistency: largely consistent across flores and xsafety, both featuring negation or opposition semantics
